title: Recover hosted next/previous and surface load-preparation truth
summary: Fixed the hosted player stepping path so next and previous can advance through the real playlist again, preserved hosted playlists across reloads, and added truthful loading-versus-failure state for media that never prepares.
reasoning: The first clean-room runtime checkpoint already played and scrubbed, so the next release needed to target the smallest real user-facing regressions instead of widening the repo again. The strongest live failures were next/previous doing nothing and supported-looking media failing without honest feedback. This version keeps the fix set inside the current player runtime and release lane so it is worth a real 0.1.4 test without dragging Meta, shells, or older seams back in.

changes.summary: Routed hosted next/previous through the hosted load path instead of the non-hosted write path.
changes.reasoning: The attached CUA player already had a valid hosted load seam; next and previous were bypassing it, which made stepping fail even though play, scrub, and skip worked.

changes.summary: Preserved hosted playlists across reloads by copying requested paths before clearing the record playlist, and filtered hosted playlists to supported video entries.
changes.reasoning: The hosted load path was collapsing its own playlist to a single item when the same list instance was passed back into reload, which left next and previous with no truthful place to go.

changes.summary: Added async prepare-pending tracking, timeout-based prepare failure reporting, and VideoPlayer error capture to the standalone player runtime.
changes.reasoning: Prepare() accepting a URL is not the same as the media actually preparing. The runtime needed an honest loading state and a bounded failure surface for files that look supported but never become playable.

changes.summary: Updated the visible player state to show loading while media is still preparing instead of collapsing to ok too early.
changes.reasoning: A truthful operator surface matters for this lane because the user is testing manually in VaM and cannot afford another silent-failure cycle.

changes.summary: Excluded runtime helper surfaces from local screen-bound calculations.
changes.reasoning: This is a small safe guardrail against the runtime helper plane polluting later surface measurements while the larger screen-core presentation seam is still being recovered.

verification: Build and deploy fa_cua_player.0.1.4.dll and fa_player_asset.0.1.4.assetbundle.
Validate the 0.1.4 release packet, including changelog artifacts and versioned live outputs.
knownIssues: The screen-core presentation seam still needs a deeper aspect and surface-stack recovery pass.
Hosted phase-1 playback remains video-only; image loading in the same lane is not restored yet.
